roster_detail.name: Ja'Marr Chase
roster_detail.scouting: Bullish: the overall WR1 - leads all receivers in FPG at high route share with elite career target rate; only risk is Burrow/CIN health.
roster_detail.film: “Jamar Chase finished as the wide receiver four in points per game after being selected as the wide receiver 21 in underdog drafts.” (@joshnorris) | “So he's an X receiver, allows us to live out of 11 personnel, and move these guys like JSN and Jamar Chase around.” (@mattharmon_byb)

tweets.t:
Ja'Marr Chase: "One of my best stories ever: Les Miles told me I couldn't play receiver when I was coming out of high school. That was something I had on my shoulders growing up. Les Miles told me he thought I could play cornerback..."

htt

Based on the sentiment I've seen, I think we're seeing a pretty egregious overreaction to the Rashee stuff for 2026 purposes. 

The jail + knee issue combo of course isn't ideal but also isn't going to stop him from being available/healthy

roster_detail.name: Brock Bowers
roster_detail.scouting: Bullish: elite every-down TE (top-3 red-zone expected points, premier first-down rate) - a tier-of-one-caliber producer capped only by LV's offense.
roster_detail.film: “Yeah, you talk about Brock Bowers and the Raiders, Mike, are expecting Bowers to bounce back in a big way.” (@dhananizain) | “Now, Tyler Linderbaum, his new teammate, the center coming over from Baltimore, said Bowers is a guy that doesn't talk much.” (@dhananizain)

tweets.t:
When the Raiders used play action last year, Tre Tucker's first-read target and air yards shares spiked.

First-read target share: 16.6% -> 28.9%

Air yards share:  24.1% -> 40.1%

Both marks top those of Brock Bowers, within this spl

Raiders will use 12 personnel a lot - they are excited about their heavy sets (Raiders version this year might be 22 personnel)

Brock Bowers will be the focal point for the offense as a chain mover (as we know)

Not worried about not havin

-Brock Bowers
-Mark Andrews

On Tre Tucker:

“If you’re wondering who’s going to rise within a young receiver unit to complement All-Pro tight end Brock Bowers in the passing attack, Tucker is the early answer ahead of mandatory minicamp.”

roster_detail.name: Cam Skattebo
roster_detail.scouting: Bearish lean: fibula-fracture recovery (effectiveness historically drops) into a contested NYG backfield; the bull case is pure Roman-scheme volume.

board.name: Justin Herbert
board.scouting: Mixed / trap: a big arm with sneaky scramble rushing, but the board flags him a trap (prefers Michael Wilson at cost) - a fine but unexciting QB.

Very logical footwork change for Justin Herbert - setting up with his left foot forward in shotgun instead of his right

board.name: Matthew Stafford

Looks much more fluid now https://t.co/H8r7KilEXR

board.name: Sam LaPorta
board.scouting: Bullish bounce-back TE in a strong DET offense; also completes the CHI@DET anchor as a DET piece.

Sam LaPorta has incredible fantasy potential with Drew Petzing calling plays https://t.co/zIsCjlSpJt

board.name: Michael Wilson
board.scouting: Mixed: bull case is a Puka-like ARI role plus the playbook's core-conviction/contingency tag, but he was an afterthought when MHJ was healthy - usage-dependent.

flags.note: Leveraged insurance: +21.5 pts if Marvin Harrison misses time. §9

board.quote: Making the math work on Trey McBride, Marvin Harrison, and Michael Wilson with Jeremiyah Love in town and Jacoby Brisset

tweets.t: Why draft Michael Wilson when you can get Jerry Jeudy 8 rounds later
RT @theo_mackie: Mike LaFleur said Marv will play the Davante Adams role as the X receiver, with Michael Wilson “closer to the core” in the…
I expect Michael Wilson to run more routes that have the higher TPRR bars in a “Z” role
This is a big difference from how it was last year - Michael Wilson as the move piece is a more prominent role

Michael Wilson kept roughly the same route distribution w/ Brissett when MHJ was out vs on the field.

Before MHJ was hurt, Wilson was an afterthought (15.8% 1st Read).

That spiked when MHJ was out (42.0% 1st Read).

Then they split who wa

board.name: Patrick Mahomes
board.scouting: Mixed: an elite ceiling but rehabbing a December ACL off a down, less-accurate season - a discounted bet with rust risk.
board.tweet: “And some men have Patrick Mahomes past ADP.” (@patkerrane)
board.film: “A week ago, he's the back of Mahomes, double clutch, in traffic, and caught on the run where she raced through the secondary to the 42-yard line.” (@superrnova38) | “And some men have Patrick Mahomes past ADP.” (@patkerrane)

Patrick Mahomes cleared for full practice soon - should be good for WK1

Kenneth Walker’s work ethic…

He was the QB2 in fantasy points per game (21.2) befor…

Just out there knocking the stuffing out of straw men.

"Justin Herbert is no Patrick Mahomes!"

Yeah, no shit.

board.name: Chris Godwin Jr.
board.scouting: Mixed: a proven slot producer and W17 stack piece, but a fibula-fracture recovery (effectiveness historically drops) is the overhang.

You guys aren’t ready for him outscoring Emeka Egbuka this year

Now, he’s spending this o…

Now, he’s spending this offseason training for performance instead of rehabbing, which matters because we’ve already seen him bounce back well

Todd Bowles addressing Chris Godwin and his health:

“It’s important that he’s been healthy,” coach Todd Bowles said. 

“Obviously, his knowledge of the game can help the younger guys, but his route-running looks sharp. He looks smooth runn

board.name: RJ Harvey
board.scouting: Bullish dart: smash upside at his late ADP if he repeats his rookie form, but Sean Payton's committee (Dobbins/Coleman) and carry caps are the risk.
board.tweet: “I deserve to be made fun of for this one, but if RJ Harvey just repeats the exact season he had as a rookie at a 7/8 turn ADP, he'd be a smash best ball pick” (@ryanj_heath)
board.film: “But I'm probably going to be wrong because my guess is Harvey is going to be the third down back.” (@dhananizain) | “Harvey went on a really nice run in terms of fantasy.” (@joshnorris)

tweets.t:

board.name: Brock Purdy
board.scouting: Bullish on efficiency: most accurate deep passer in 2025 and top EPA/dropback QB three years running, top-10 FP/G each year, now with an upgraded WR room — but a pocket passer with limited rushing, so a high-floor QB that lacks the dual-threat ceiling this build prizes.
board.quote: Lowest % of dropbacks targeting an "open" receiver (not just a step, not a busted coverage) Dak Prescott - 39.9% Brock P
board.tweet: “RT @DBro_FFB: Brock Purdy over the last 3 yrs in fppg QB5 QB10 QB6 and YET QB14-QB16 in Best Ball depending upon the site that you're dra…” (@dbro_ffb) | “Brock Purdy over the last 3 yrs in fppg QB5 QB10 QB6 and YET QB14-QB16 in Best Ball depending upon the site that you're drafting on.” (@dbro_ffb)

“The chemistry between Evans and Purdy has already been evident during OTAs”

Relationships matter for fantasy

board.name: Michael Pittman Jr.
board.scouting: Mixed: a high-target possession WR in a McCarthy 3-WR scheme (PIT); volume-based value with a ceiling capped by iffy QB play.

board.name: Rico Dowdle
board.scouting: Mixed: a committee back in PIT (the lightning to Warren's thunder) - efficient, but capped touches limit the ceiling.

board.name: Harold Fannin Jr.
board.scouting: Bullish sleeper TE: flashed an elite TE1 workload without Njoku and draws rave film reviews; the cap is sharing targets with Njoku when healthy.

board.name: Jayden Reed
board.scouting: Mixed: explosive but undersized GB slot with boom weeks; a crowded target tree and inconsistency cap him to a dart.

board.name: Jaxson Dart
board.scouting: Bullish: the playbook's max-conviction QB - an efficient young dual-threat with rising hype; the cap is a developing NYG supporting cast.

board.name: Blake Corum
board.scouting: Mixed / trap: a productive-with-a-lead LAR back and Kyren handcuff with breakout buzz, but the board flags him a trap (prefers Dart at cost).

flags.note: Trap — take Jaxson Dart instead (+23 board ranks). §4.2

board.name: Kenneth Gainwell
board.scouting: Mixed contingency: rising on Bucky Irving injury buzz into a TB passing-down role; a dart whose value spikes mainly if Irving misses time.

board.name: Jared Goff
board.scouting: Stable, accurate pocket passer in an elite DET offense (great supporting cast) — high floor, but zero rushing means a capped ceiling in a build that prizes dual-threat QBs.

board.name: Josh Downs
board.scouting: Bullish sleeper: elite target-rate efficiency with a role set to expand after IND's WR departures; QB play is the only cap.

board.name: George Kittle
board.scouting: Bullish-with-risk: still one of the most efficient TEs (elite first-down rate/YPRR), but an Achilles flag and age cloud a high ceiling.

flags.note: Achilles; 61.9% inline negative indicator — verify camp. §11

board.name: Kyle Monangai
board.scouting: Bullish sleeper RB: trending up (~50% of the CHI backfield late) with sharp buzz to beat his ADP; a committee/role risk remains.

board.name: Jordan Addison
board.scouting: Mixed: a talented MIN WR2 with efficiency, but target competition (JJ) and a drops flag cap him to a WR3/4 value.

board.name: Jakobi Meyers
board.scouting: Mixed / trap: a steady target hog central to JAX's passing, but the board flags him a trap (prefers Dart) - high floor, low ceiling.

board.name: Jonathon Brooks
board.scouting: Bullish-volatile: real buzz he becomes the CAR lead over Hubbard if healthy, but he is coming off a major injury - a high-upside dart.

board.name: J.K. Dobbins
board.scouting: Mixed: looked healthy again and could lead the DEN backfield, but Sean Payton's committee (Harvey/Coleman) caps the workload.

board.name: Jordan Love
board.scouting: Mixed: a clean deep-ball passer with a good pocket, but a middling rushing floor and some overpriced takes cap the QB upside.

board.name: Bo Nix
board.scouting: Mixed / trap: a steady DEN passer, but limited rushing and the board flags him a trap (prefers Murray) - capped QB upside.

board.name: Dalton Kincaid
board.scouting: Mixed / trap: efficient when on the field, but injury-prone and route-share-limited; the board flags him a trap (prefers Murray).

board.name: Ricky Pearsall
board.scouting: Mixed: an ascending SF WR with contingency upside, but missed OTA time and a crowded target tree temper him.

board.name: Matthew Golden
board.scouting: Mixed rookie WR: an ascending GB pass-catcher with upside, but a crowded target tree and rookie variance cap the role.

board.name: Wan'Dale Robinson
board.scouting: Mixed: elite reception volume (90+ catches twice) but a small-bodied, low-aDOT profile caps spike weeks - PPR floor over ceiling.

board.name: Xavier Worthy
board.scouting: Mixed: an explosive KC field-stretcher with WR1 upside if the hierarchy breaks his way; boom-or-bust on volume.

board.name: Tyler Shough
board.scouting: Bullish sleeper QB: a buzzy Kellen Moore fit in NO with a real running game - a cheap late dart with starter upside.

board.name: Kyler Murray
board.scouting: Mixed: dual-threat upside if he wins the MIN job (the Jefferson-build insurance per the playbook), but the starting role isn't locked - a conditional dart.

board.name: Travis Kelce
board.scouting: Bearish-lean: an aging KC TE past his peak (changing of the guard at the position) - name value over current production.

board.name: Rachaad White
board.scouting: Mixed: a pass-catching committee back (WAS) with a PPR floor but little standalone ceiling.

board.name: Romeo Doubs
board.scouting: Bearish: a low-upside complementary WR sharp takes dismiss - a deep dart only.

board.name: Baker Mayfield
board.scouting: Bullish value: an accurate, productive TB passer and the playbook's 2A stack anchor (a window-winner at ADP) - a strong cheap QB.

board.name: Khalil Shakir
board.scouting: Bullish-lean: a high-floor BUF target earner (strong designed-target volume) - a safe PPR role with a modest ceiling.

board.name: Aaron Jones Sr.
board.scouting: Mixed: still useful MIN volume, but age and a crowded/uncertain backfield cap him to a dart.

board.name: Dallas Goedert
board.scouting: Bullish sleeper TE: highly efficient (top PHI YPRR, especially without A.J. Brown) - a value TE if healthy and targeted.

board.name: Isaiah Likely
board.scouting: Mixed / trap: a talented BAL TE with breakout flashes, but stuck behind Andrews and flagged a trap (prefers Mayfield).

board.name: Jacory Croskey-Merritt
board.scouting: Mixed rookie RB: efficient flashes in a muddled WAS backfield - a contingency dart.

board.name: KC Concepcion
board.scouting: Speculative rookie WR: a deep dart in a crowded CLE room - an upside bet only.

board.name: Stefon Diggs
board.scouting: Mixed: a savvy, cheap veteran target earner (NE), but age and injury recovery cap the ceiling - decent floor value.

board.name: Sam Darnold
board.scouting: Mixed-bullish: a high passer-rating game-manager in a good SEA scheme, but a low rushing floor caps the QB ceiling.

board.name: Jake Ferguson
board.scouting: Bullish-lean: a target-secure DAL TE with strong red-zone usage - a steady mid-tier TE value.

board.name: Jalen Coker
board.scouting: Bullish sleeper: elite efficiency (top EPA/target, first-read passer rating) - a strong CAR breakout dart if the volume comes.

board.name: C.J. Stroud
board.scouting: Mixed: a talented young HOU passer with deep-ball upside, but a 2025 step-back and limited rushing temper the outlook.

board.name: Jordan Mason
board.scouting: Mixed: a competent MIN backup RB with contingency value behind Jones - a dart unless he wins more work.

board.name: Malik Willis
board.scouting: Bearish: a low-volume backup-caliber QB - a deep dart with no real role barring an injury.

board.name: Chig Okonkwo
board.scouting: Bullish late dart: a popular cheap TE breakout pick with athletic upside; the ceiling rides a target bump.

board.name: Jayden Higgins
board.scouting: Bullish sleeper rookie WR: HOU coaches are high on him (trending up) - an upside dart behind the established options.

board.name: Daniel Jones
board.scouting: Mixed: a mobile QB with rushing-floor appeal (IND), but accuracy and role questions cap the ceiling - a streamer dart.

board.name: Mark Andrews
board.scouting: Mixed: a proven red-zone BAL TE, but age and a fibula-recovery flag temper the ceiling.

board.name: Tyrone Tracy Jr.
board.scouting: Mixed: an efficient receiving back but a poor fit for the Roman power scheme (Skattebo's the fit); the board still values him over the traps.

board.name: Chris Rodriguez Jr.
board.scouting: Mixed: an early-down/short-yardage JAX back - touchdown-dependent with no passing-down role.

board.name: Zach Charbonnet
board.scouting: Bullish: positioned for a lead SEA role with Walker gone to KC - real standalone upside and a board window-winner.

board.name: Cam Ward
board.scouting: Mixed / trap: a high-pedigree rookie QB on a weak TEN roster; the board flags him a trap (prefers Tracy) - dynasty over 2026.

flags.note: Trap — take Tyrone Tracy Jr. instead (+20). §4.2

board.name: Jalen McMillan
board.scouting: Mixed: the TB WR3 with spike upside if injuries hit ahead of him - a contingency dart.

board.name: Brenton Strange
board.scouting: Mixed: a developing JAX receiving TE behind a deep pass-catch group - a deep dart.

board.name: Keaton Mitchell
board.scouting: Speculative: an explosive change-of-pace back with contingency upside - a late dart that needs an injury to matter.

board.name: Oronde Gadsden II
board.scouting: Mixed sleeper TE: receiving upside in a multi-TE LAC scheme, but a poor blocker - a usage-dependent dart.

board.name: Travis Hunter
board.scouting: Mixed: rare two-way talent, but uncertain WR snap/usage caps the floor - an upside dart.

board.name: Isiah Pacheco
board.scouting: Mixed: a physical committee back with a fibula-recovery flag - contingency value with a capped workload.

board.name: Rashid Shaheed
board.scouting: Bullish sleeper: a deep-threat OTA standout (SEA) with explosive upside - a cheap field-stretcher dart.

board.name: Woody Marks
board.scouting: Mixed rookie RB: a pass-catching HOU back the board likes as a window-value; the standalone role is the question.

board.name: Hunter Henry
board.scouting: Bullish value: a target-secure NE TE and board window-winner with a Maye-driven floor - a steady cheap TE.

board.name: Kenyon Sadiq
board.scouting: Speculative rookie TE: athletic traits but raw - a deep dynasty/dart bet.

board.name: Bryce Young
board.scouting: Bearish: an inaccurate deep passer capping the CAR offense (the reason McMillan is held back) - a low QB ceiling.

board.name: Omar Cooper Jr.
board.scouting: Speculative rookie WR: a deep NYJ dart - an upside bet behind the established options.

board.name: Jauan Jennings
board.scouting: Mixed: a reliable SF possession WR with target volume, but a capped ceiling in a crowded offense.

board.name: Jalen Nailor
board.scouting: Speculative: a deep MIN field-stretcher behind Jefferson/Addison - dart only.

board.name: Denzel Boston
board.scouting: Speculative rookie WR dart - thin signal.

board.name: Aaron Rodgers
board.scouting: Bearish: an aging QB off injury-marred years (PIT) - low ceiling, streamer at best.

board.name: Tyjae Spears
board.scouting: Mixed: a TEN committee back battling for the RB2 role - a contingency dart.

board.name: Tyler Allgeier
board.scouting: Mixed: an efficient ATL early-down backup - contingency value behind Bijan.

board.name: Ryan Flournoy
board.scouting: Speculative: flashed efficiency in limited DAL routes - a deep dart, volume the question.

board.name: Tre Tucker
board.scouting: Mixed: an ascending LV speed WR with a defined role - upside dart in a weak offense.

board.name: Malik Washington
board.scouting: Mixed: an ascending MIA slot WR - a PPR dart with role-growth upside.

board.name: Dalton Schultz
board.scouting: Mixed: a target-secure HOU TE with occasional spikes - a low-ceiling streamer.

board.name: Alvin Kamara
board.scouting: Mixed: still a useful pass-catching NO back, but age and offense decline cap him - a PPR dart.

board.name: Antonio Williams
board.scouting: Speculative: a depth WR dart with thin coachspeak - role-dependent.

board.name: Deebo Samuel Sr.
board.scouting: Mixed: a versatile YAC weapon but an aging free agent - role/landing-spot-dependent dart.

board.name: Jacoby Brissett
board.scouting: Mixed: the named ARI starter with a decent supporting cast - a streamer if the job holds.

board.name: Jonah Coleman
board.scouting: Mixed: a DEN committee dart in Payton's rotation - contingency only.

board.name: Fernando Mendoza
board.scouting: Speculative rookie QB - dynasty/dart only, no 2026 role expected.

board.name: Isaac TeSlaa
board.scouting: Speculative: a deep DET WR dart with developmental upside.

board.name: Greg Dulcich
board.scouting: Speculative: an athletic TE with a thin role - a dart.

board.name: Gunnar Helm
board.scouting: Speculative rookie TE: TEN coaches like him - a developmental dart.

board.name: Dylan Sampson
board.scouting: Bullish sleeper RB: elite receiving efficiency (top YPRR) - a strong CLE pass-down dart with upside.

board.name: AJ Barner
board.scouting: Mixed: a blocking-first SEA TE with red-zone sneak upside - a deep streamer.

board.name: Brian Robinson Jr.
board.scouting: Mixed: a physical contact-balance back - touchdown-dependent committee value.

board.name: Tank Bigsby
board.scouting: Mixed: a committee/backup RB (PHI) - a contingency dart behind Saquon.

board.name: Tyreek Hill
board.scouting: Mixed: still an elite route-runner but a free agent - upside contingent on landing spot and age.

board.name: Tre' Harris

board.name: Adonai Mitchell

board.name: De'Zhaun Stribling

board.scouting: Speculative: a TB depth back - contingency dart behind Irving.

board.scouting: mixed (2↑/1↓, 3 src): targets/volume, breakout/ceiling.

board.name: Justin Fields
board.scouting: Bullish late: the #1 QB in rushing XFP and the playbook's mandatory late add - an elite rushing floor at minimal cost.
